channel.title: Morning Mail
channel.description: Stay updated on the latest news and events happening in the RPI community.

item.title: Schmidt Science Fellowship Seeking Applicants for Prestigious Post-doctoral Fellowship
item.description: Fellows receive $110K per year for two years, are placed at an internationally prestigious lab, and are mentored by world leaders in science and industry. PhD students graduating between May 2026 and June 2027 who are interested in developing skills in a discipline different than their own should contact Betty Madigan, madige@rpi.edu, by April 10th for information on the internal selection process. A committee will select 2 nominees to be submitted in May. Questions? madige@rpi.edu
item.author: Students

item.title: Volunteer with the Sidewalk Warriors!
item.description: On Thursday, April 2, 2026, beginning at 4:30 PM, the CCPD and participating students and alumni will be volunteering with Sidewalk Warriors in Troy, New York. See below for information on how you can volunteer! Register in Handshake: https://rpi.joinhandshake.com/events/1890471/share_preview
item.author: Students

item.title: Competitions for Graduate School Salary and Tuition Info Session March 18th 10am
item.description: Date: Wednesday, March 18, 2026 10:00 AM - 11:00 AM - Competitive fellowships provide a salary and tuition while you earn a graduate degree. Graduate students, seniors, and juniors: come learn how to find and apply to programs that will fund fall 2027 and beyond. Programs are available at all stages of your academic career. International students are welcome! Register for the virtual session. https://surveys.rpi.edu/jfe/form/SV_6L3FFwv0qCROrvE Email madige@rpi.edu for more information.
item.author: Students

item.title: ECSE Topical Seminar: “Toward a Calculus for Compound AI to Understand Complex Systems”
item.description: Date: Monday, March 9, 2026 4:00 PM - 5:00 PM - Join us for an ECSE Topical Seminar: “Toward a Calculus for Compound AI to Understand Complex Systems” with Lav Varshney of Stony Brook University. Abstract and speaker bio can be found at the event URL.
item.author: Community

item.title: Student Experience Excellence Awards
item.description: The Student Experience Excellence Awards (formerly the Graduating Student Awards) recognize RPI students who have made exceptional contributions to our community through academic achievement, athletic accomplishment, leadership, service, and/or volunteerism. We invite students, staff, and faculty to nominate individuals who exemplify excellence and have made a meaningful impact on our campus.
item.author: Community

item.title: DotCIO - Server Maintenance Scheduled starting 9am on 03/07/2026
item.description: Due to necessary software maintenance, a scheduled outage will take place beginning at 9:00AM on Saturday, March 7th with an expected duration of 3-5 hours. During this time, some computing services may be unavailable. It should be noted that this will NOT be a network outage. Please click in for additional details and link to report if issues are experienced after this time frame.
item.author: Community

item.title: Zendesk ticketing system requiring email verification effective 03/09/2026
item.description: In an effort to prevent spam attacks, effective 03/09/2026 the Zendesk ticketing system is requiring a one time email verification for each instance for those who submit requests and are not signed into the system. This new workflow will require anonymous (not signed‑in) individuals who submit requests to verify their email address before an active ticket is created.
item.author: Community

item.title: A Non-Variational Quantum Approach to Solving Combinatorial Optimization Problems: Iterative-QAOA
item.description: Date: Thursday, March 5, 2026 12:00 PM - 1:00 PM - Quantum computers introduce new opportunities for modeling and solving decision problems. In this talk, we will first discuss the unique positioning of Industrial Engineering in the quantum research landscape that motivates new directions at the intersection of application and theory. Then as an example, we will present a new algorithm, Iterative-QAOA, for combinational optimization problems.
item.author: Community

item.title: Exciting News: Ice Rink Project at Houston Field House Begins March 6
item.description: Dear Campus Community, We are pleased to announce that the long-anticipated ice rink renovation project at the Houston Field House at Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute will officially commence on Friday, March 6, with substantial completion anticipated by early September. This significant investment represents an exciting new chapter for our campus and for RPI’s proud and competitive hockey tradition. The upgraded rink and supporting systems will enhance performance, safety, and the overall…
item.author: Community

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Live Course: “Leading Hybrid Teams That Actually Work”
item.description: Hybrid work is no longer a policy decision—it’s a leadership capability. Many leaders are managing hybrid teams using outdated assumptions from either fully in-office or fully remote environments, resulting in friction, slow decisions, and uneven performance. This Leadercamp helps leaders shift from generic hybrid rules to intentional practices that build clarity, trust, and results. Two times: 9:00 AM - 10:00 AM and 12:00 PM- 1:00 PM.
item.author: Faculty and Staff

item.title: Division of Human Resources Professional Development Live Course: “How to Stay Motivated When Work Gets Tough” March 10, 2026 9:00 AM- 10:00 AM & 12:00 PM- 1:00 PM
item.description:
Motivation often breaks down under pressure, when work becomes difficult, demanding, or uncertain. During this Leadercamp, participants will learn how to clarify what matters most, reconnect effort to identity and purpose, and design environments that support sustained performance. Through examples, reflection, and applied tools, attendees will understand how to navigate challenging work phases, maintain focus when progress feels slow, and continue moving forward without burnout.

For information about sharing your news or events, or to change your subscription settings, visit morningmail.rpi.edu.
item.author: Faculty and Staff
